exemplar.title:
How does Colleen Hoover explore emotional trauma through the
use of imagery, motifs and dialogue through the novel It Ends with Us?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does Colleen Hoover explore emotional trauma through the
use of imagery, motifs and dialogue through the novel It Ends with Us?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to each section of the discussion
Effective signposting and section headings maintain a clear sense of direction throughout
Language is generally precise and the purpose of analysing imagery, motifs and dialogue is evident from the outset
weaknesses: Methodology is functional but lacks a detailed rationale for source and quote selection
Occasional contextual detours (e.g. Hooverâs popularity, film adaptation) dilute the tight focus on the RQ
Minor imprecisions in wording (âsymbolisticâ, âprolificanceâ) slightly weaken clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The criterion falls solidly in the 5â6 band: the topic is communicated effectively and the research question is both focused and consistently tied to the analysis. The methodology is outlined and generally relevant but not fully robust, preventing a topâband award. Occasional contextual asides and minor linguistic slips keep the overall score at 5 rather than 6.

strengths: Accurate recounting of the novelâs plot and selection of pertinent textual evidence demonstrate clear subject knowledge
Correct use of core literary terminology (imagery, motif, dialogue, metaphor) in most instances
Coherent understanding of the theme of emotional trauma is evident throughout
weaknesses: Engagement with scholarly sources is limited; reliance on popular journalism weakens theoretical depth
Definitions of key concepts are simplistic and drawn from nonâacademic web sources
Occasional imprecision in terminology (e.g. nonâstandard term âsymbolisticâ) indicates inconsistent conceptual control
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and Understanding is placed in the mid-band (3â4) because while the student shows clear and accurate understanding of the novel and its devices, the application of sources and depth of conceptual grounding fall short of the highest bands. The work is coherent and competent but not sufficiently sophisticated to merit above a 3.

strengths: Substantial and consistently relevant primary research with well-chosen quotations from the novel
Evidence of close reading in passages that effectively support the analysis of emotional trauma
A generally coherent line of reasoning linking textual evidence back to the research question
weaknesses: Over-reliance on descriptive summary rather than deep analytical interrogation in places
Secondary research is limited in scope and scholarly depth, relying mostly on journalistic sources
Evaluation of techniques and implications is present but not fully developed into a critical framework
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking merits a mid-band score of 5. The student demonstrates strong primary research and some effective analysis, but the argument is at times descriptive, and engagement with scholarly criticism and evaluative depth is only partial. These factors keep the work below the top of the 4â6 band yet above the lower threshold.

strengths: All required structural elements (title page, table of contents, clear section headings, pagination) are present and correctly applied
Logical progression from introduction through body sections to conclusion enhances readability
Consistent formatting and neat presentation contribute to a professional appearance
weaknesses: Citation and works-cited formatting show minor inconsistencies in punctuation and line breaks
Page ranges in the table of contents do not always match actual section breaks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the descriptors for the top band (3â4): structure and formatting are clearly appropriate and reader-friendly. Minor lapses in citation style and navigational precision prevent any deduction, so the full mark of 4 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Method is outlined but only partially developed.** The student explains that a literary analysis of selected quotations will be undertaken and mentions the use of âa couple [of] secondary sourcesâ. Primary-source engagement is clear, yet the choice and range of secondary material are limited and largely journalistic rather than scholarly.

**Evidence of informed selection is uneven.** The quotations from the novel are pertinent, but the rationale for choosing particular episodes or critical sources is not explained; nor is there sustained dialogue with literary scholarship. Citations are present but inconsistent in format and occasionally incomplete.

**Overall, the methodology is functional rather than fully robust.** It satisfies the descriptors of a âmostly complete methodology with generally relevant sourcesâ (band 3â4). However, the limited breadth and depth of secondary research and the absence of detailed methodological justification place it at the lower end of that bandâhence a score of 3.

**Most formal presentation requirements are met.** The essay includes a word count, title, table of contents, page numbers and a works-cited list. Line-spacing and font size appear standard and consistent, supporting readability.

**Some inconsistencies reduce precision.** Citation style (presumably MLA) shows lapses in punctuation and formatting; in-text citations are sometimes incomplete (missing page numbers for web sources or lacking publication details). Page references in the table of contents do not perfectly match section breaks.

**Overall conformity with minor errors.** Given that layout conventions are largely but not flawlessly applied, the work fits best at the lower end of the 3-4 band, earning a score of 3.

**Primary research is substantial and always relevant.** Numerous well-selected quotations from *It Ends with Us* directly address the research question, demonstrating purposeful engagement with the core text.

**Secondary research is present but limited.** The bibliography lists a handful of journalistic and web-based commentaries plus a few definitions; peer-reviewed literary scholarship is largely absent. Nevertheless, the sources used relate to Hoover, trauma, and literary terminology, providing adequate contextual support.

**Satisfies âadequateâ to âgoodâ descriptors.** Given the strong primary focus but modest critical range, the work aligns with the 4â6 band for research quality. The generally relevant though not comprehensive research justifies a mid-band score of 5.

**Logical structure with emerging argument.** The essay moves from definition to three technique-based sections and a summative conclusion, providing a discernible line of reasoning about how trauma is conveyed.

**Evaluation is present but limited.** The student occasionally reflects on the effectiveness of Hooverâs techniques (e.g., noting discomfort caused by vivid imagery, acknowledging possible oversimplification of trauma), yet these reflections are brief and not fully integrated into a critical framework.

**Partial critical engagement.** While the argument is coherent, it lacks sustained evaluation of perspectives or deeper implications. This places the work securely in the 4â6 band for discussion/evaluation, at a lower score of 4.

**Evidence of analytical effort.** The student interprets specific passages to illustrate how imagery, motifs and dialogue reveal trauma, often unpacking diction and juxtaposition. This shows awareness of how literary devices function.

**Analysis tends toward description.** Extended plot summary and paraphrase frequently substitute for deeper textual interrogation. Connections to the research question are asserted rather than critically demonstrated, and alternative readings or counter-arguments are absent.

**Support is uneven.** Claims are usually backed by quotations, but the reasoning sometimes remains superficial, leading to partially supported conclusions. These qualities meet the lower end of the 4â6 band; thus a score of 4 is warranted.

**All required structural elements are included.** The essay contains a title page, abstract-style introduction, table of contents, clearly labelled sections, conclusion and works cited. Logical progression from introduction through three body sections to conclusion demonstrates appropriate organisation.

**Headings and pagination aid navigation.** The table of contents aligns closely with section headings and page numbers, permitting easy location of material. Paragraphing is mostly coherent, and visual presentation is neat and reader-friendly.

**Minor lapses only.** Some sections overrun the page ranges listed, and sub-headings are inconsistently formatted, but these issues are minor. The work comfortably meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band and merits the top mark of 4.

**Demonstrates solid but not outstanding subject knowledge.** The student recounts the plot accurately, defines key literary devices and selects apt textual evidence, showing a clear, if conventional, understanding of Hooverâs novel and the theme of trauma.

**Use of sources is only partially effective.** While primary evidence is plentiful, engagement with critical or theoretical literature is thin and mostly limited to popular journalism. As a result, the essay relies heavily on narrative summary and personal commentary rather than sustained scholarly dialogue.

**Competency outweighs originality.** The understanding is coherent and mostly accurate, situating the novel within contemporary literature, yet it lacks the depth and range expected for the highest band. These qualities fit the 3â4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

**Terminology is generally accurate and relevant.** The student employs core literary termsâimagery, motif, dialogue, metaphor, symbolismâcorrectly in most cases, indicating a reasonable grasp of English A: Language & Literature concepts.

**Inconsistencies and occasional misuse.** There are moments of imprecision (âsymbolisticâ, âprolificanceâ) and some definitions are simplistic or drawn from non-academic web sources, revealing gaps in sophistication.

**Overall control but lacking consistent precision.** The work meets the âmostly accurateâ criterion of the 3â4 band rather than the sustained accuracy and nuance required for 5â6. A score of 3 best reflects the mixed but competent command of terminology.

**Research question is explicit, focused and literary in nature.** The student states: âHow does Colleen Hoover explore emotional trauma through the use of imagery, motifs and dialogueâ¦?â This question is clearly articulated, narrow enough for in-depth treatment, and closely aligned with the subject.

**Frequent referencing back to the RQ.** Each main body section is organised around one of the three specified techniques, maintaining an ongoing link to the question. Topic sentences and concluding statements regularly invoke the aim of showing how trauma is communicated.

**Some uneven integration lowers the score within the top band.** While the focus is usually maintained, there are momentsâparticularly in the introduction and conclusionâwhere broader contextual commentary (e.g., Hooverâs popularity, film adaptation, trauma in 21st-century literature) dilutes the tight linkage to the research question. Consequently the strand merits a strong 5 rather than a 6.

annotations.comment: Extended plot paraphrase replaces critical evaluation; develop an argument about how these incidents shape thematic progression rather than retell events.
The use of detailed textual quotation here powerfully conveys Lilyâs internal conflict; this precise evidence effectively supports your analysis of entrapment.
Clear section heading for the motifs analysis provides effective structural signposting and prepares the reader for the shift in focus.
Transitional sentence feels generic; refine to explicitly link motif discussion to upcoming dialogue section and maintain argumentative flow.
Labelling this as the third body paragraph helps the reader track the essayâs structure and aligns with your stated methodology.
Dialogue analysis is detailed but stops short of evaluating its broader impact on reader understanding; discuss how these moments shift narrative perspective.
Conclusion largely reiterates body content without synthesizing arguments or suggesting implications; aim for a more evaluative synthesis of findings.
Discussion of character development remains superficial; integrate more nuanced literary theory to frame Lilyâs emotional arc analytically.
Works cited entries show inconsistent formatting (italics, punctuation, line breaks); apply one style guide rigorously for all references.
Last works-cited entry is orphaned from its preceding author information; ensure each entry is continuous without split lines.
Interpretation of motifs is insightful but would benefit from secondary theoretical perspectives to deepen the critical context.
Definition of emotional trauma lacks engagement with academic theory; cite a peer-reviewed source to strengthen conceptual grounding.
Page ranges in the table of contents do not precisely match section breaks in the text, reducing navigational clarity; update these to reflect actual pagination.
The contextual detour into Hooverâs popularity and film adaptation, while interesting, dilutes focus on the research question; tighten the introduction to maintain relevance.
Reliance on journalistic quotes for contextual evidence limits scholarly depth; integrate peer-reviewed criticism to bolster research relevance.
Methodology description is too generalâlacking rationale for source selection and criteria for quote choice; explicitly justify your methodological decisions.
Analysis here remains descriptiveâunpack the connotations of the imagery and link them explicitly to Lilyâs psychological state for a deeper reading.
This paragraph is overly long and covers multiple motifs at once; consider breaking it into focused subsections to improve readability and coherence.
Terminology definitions are drawn from non-academic web sources and remain simplistic; replace with scholarly definitions to demonstrate deeper conceptual mastery.
Your analysis of interior monologue effectively reveals Lilyâs internalised trauma; this close reading demonstrates good critical engagement with the text.
The conclusion is clearly labelled and completes the formal structure as outlined in your table of contents.
Hyperlink placement and line breaks are uneven; adjust each reference block so that URLs remain attached to their corresponding citation.
The opening analysis of imagery provides summary rather than critical interrogation; focus on how specific sensory details function rhetorically in context of trauma.
The student communicates the topic and purpose accurately and consistently, establishing a clear focus on imagery, motifs, and dialogue to explore trauma from the outset.
The term âsymbolisticâ is imprecise and non-standard; replace with accurate literary terminology (e.g., âsymbolicâ) to demonstrate conceptual control.
Your brief acknowledgment of limitations is a positive step, but this evaluation should engage more critically with the complexity of trauma representation.
